response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, extensive leadership experience, and architectural capability, they fall critically short on the role's core technical requirements. The position explicitly requires 8 years of C++ experience for a cloud compute platform role; this resume shows only ~1 year of C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015). Their background is predominantly web services, data integration, and full-stack development using JavaScript/TypeScript and D, not systems-level infrastructure or compute platform engineering.
strengths: Proven technical leadership across multiple senior and staff roles with mentoring and workshop experience
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality) and ability to architect large-scale systems (GISCollective, distributed data integration at Dunnhumby)
Demonstrated capacity to own complex, ambiguous projects from conception through production deployment
concerns: Critical gap: Only ~6–12 months of C++ experience versus the 8-year requirement—a disqualifying deficit for a compute platform role
Primary technology stack (JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js, D) does not align with systems-level infrastructure and compute architecture needs
Limited explicit experience building or architecting cloud compute platforms; background is web services and data integration rather than infrastructure or distributed compute systems
